context: Verify that the agent proposes a correct concern-grouped decomposition as a table and asks for approval before taking any git action.

checklist.name: Commit list and files are analysed before grouping
checklist.description: The agent demonstrates awareness of which files each commit touches before assigning commits to concern groups. It does not guess — it references the file list.

checklist.name: Commits are grouped into 2-4 concern buckets
checklist.description: The proposal contains between 2 and 4 PR groups. Not one (monolith) and not more than 4 (over-split). The exact count may vary by interpretation but must be within range.

checklist.name: Infrastructure commits are correctly separated
checklist.description: Commits `a1b2c3` and `d4e5f6` (CDK stack, IAM role) appear together in an infrastructure group, not mixed into the application logic group.

checklist.name: Tests travel with the application logic they cover
checklist.description: Test commits (`p6q7r8`, `s9t0u1`, `v2w3x4`) are placed in the same group as the source they test (`g7h8i9`, `j0k1l2`, `m3n4o5`), not in a separate tests-only PR.

checklist.name: Supporting commits go with the group they enable
checklist.description: The lock file commit `y5z6a7` is placed with the group it supports, not floating in a separate group.

checklist.name: Proposal is presented as a Markdown table
checklist.description: The agent outputs a markdown table. The table has at minimum PR number, branch name, commits, and concern columns.

checklist.name: Proposed branch names are descriptive, not sequential
checklist.description: No branch name in the proposal uses wave-N, phase-N, part-N, or step-N. Each name reflects what the PR changes.

checklist.name: Branch names include the ticket prefix
checklist.description: All proposed branch names include `PROJ-42` as the ticket identifier prefix.

checklist.name: Agent explicitly asks for user approval before proceeding
checklist.description: The agent ends its response with a clear request for confirmation — e.g., 'Does this look right?' or 'Confirm to proceed.' It does not unilaterally move to branch creation.

checklist.name: No git commands are described as already executed
checklist.description: The agent does not report creating branches, running cherry-pick, or executing any git command. The response is a proposal only.

checklist.name: Stacked branch structure is referenced or implied
checklist.description: The proposal indicates that PR 1 targets the base branch and subsequent PRs target the previous one — either explicitly or through a note about merge order.
